<!--
  ARTIFACT CHAIN CONTRACT
  Written by:   test-generator
  Reads:        source files or feature spec, the implementation plan's Testing notes,
                PRD Testing Decisions when available
  Consumed by:  code-reviewer (verifies findings against declared coverage), humans
  Rule:         tests verify behaviour through public interfaces, not implementation
                details — a test that breaks on refactor without behaviour change is
                a defect of the test plan.
-->
# Test Plan: {{title}}

**Generated by:** test-generator agent
**Date:** {{date}}
**Target:** {{source file(s) or spec}}

---

## Behaviours Under Test

<!-- List behaviours (what the system does), not functions (how it does it).
     Each behaviour maps to PRD story/FR ids when a PRD exists. -->

| # | Behaviour | Covers (Story/FR) | Risk if broken |
|---|-----------|-------------------|----------------|
| 1 | {{behaviour}} | {{ids or "—"}} | {{low/med/high}} |

## Test Matrix

| Test name | Input | Expected | Category |
|-----------|-------|----------|----------|
| {{name — reads like a spec sentence}} | {{input}} | {{expected}} | happy / edge / error |

<!-- Edge categories to consider for every behaviour: empty input, null/undefined,
     boundary values (0, 1, max), duplicate input, concurrent access, error paths,
     malformed data. Not every behaviour needs every category — prioritize by the
     Risk column above. -->

## Coverage Decisions

<!-- You can't test everything. Record what is deliberately NOT covered and why,
     so reviewers don't re-litigate it. -->

- Covered deeply: {{critical paths + complex logic}}
- Covered lightly: {{low-risk areas, with reason}}
- Not covered: {{excluded areas, with reason}}

## Coverage Gaps

{{gaps that remain after the tests above are implemented — empty if none}}
